priors.claim: WorkRail's AgentLoop only supports sequential tool execution (toolExecution: 'sequential')
priors.notes: Stated in task context; verifiable against src/daemon/agent-loop.ts

priors.claim: Claude Code supports parallel tool execution for some tool calls
priors.notes: Confirmed by SQ1-C1, SQ1-C2, SQ1-C3, SQ1-C4. Parallel execution via partitionToolCalls + runToolsConcurrently + StreamingToolExecutor. Corroborated by multiple single-source claims from toolOrchestration.ts and StreamingToolExecutor.ts.

priors.claim: WorkRail's stuck detection uses a repeated_tool_call heuristic (same tool+args 3x in a row)
WorkRail stall detection uses a stallTimeoutMs timer that resets before each LLM call
WorkRail coordinator-spawned sessions have a delivery gap (never commit/PR/merge) -- B1+B6
priors.notes: Stated as known gap B1+B6 in task context

priors.claim: WorkRail tools THROW on failure and the AgentLoop catches them and returns isError tool_results
Claude Code has a context compaction/summarization strategy for managing context window pressure
priors.notes: Confirmed by SQ4-C4, SQ4-C5. Claude Code has FOUR layered compaction strategies (autocompact, reactive-compact, snip, context-collapse). More sophisticated than a simple summarization.

priors.claim: Claude Code uses an AbortController or similar cancellation primitive for in-flight tool calls
priors.notes: Confirmed by SQ3-C1 through SQ3-C7. Three-level AbortController hierarchy: session > sibling-batch > per-tool. More sophisticated than expected -- not a single controller but a hierarchical cascade.

priors.claim: Claude Code's parallel tool execution has a safety classification system distinguishing read-only vs mutating tools
priors.notes: Confirmed by SQ2-C1 through SQ2-C6. Four-axis classification: isConcurrencySafe, isReadOnly, isDestructive, interruptBehavior. BashTool uses dynamic per-command analysis, not static flags.

priors.claim: Claude Code handles delivery/commit flow inside the agent loop, not as a post-processing step
priors.notes: Confirmed by SQ5-C2. Workers self-verify and commit as part of their task execution ('Run relevant tests and typecheck, then commit your changes and report the hash'). No separate post-task hook -- commit is inside the worker.

priors.claim: Claude Code has more sophisticated stuck/stall detection than WorkRail (e.g., progress metrics, token velocity checks)
priors.notes: Partially falsified. Claude Code has NO dedicated stuck detector (SQ6-C1). It uses maxTurns + token-budget diminishing-returns (SQ4-C3). WorkRail's repeated_tool_call heuristic is actually comparable sophistication. The 'more sophisticated' premise is wrong -- different approaches, neither clearly superior. WorkRail lacks the token-velocity check; Claude Code lacks the exact-repeat heuristic.

priors.claim: The leaked Claude Code source is available and browsable at https://github.com/codeaashu/claude-code
priors.notes: Confirmed -- multiple source files successfully fetched from raw.githubusercontent.com/codeaashu/claude-code/main/src/. All key files (Tool.ts, tools.ts, toolOrchestration.ts, StreamingToolExecutor.ts, toolExecution.ts, query.ts, tokenBudget.ts, transitions.ts, stopHooks.ts, coordinatorMode.ts, LocalAgentTask.tsx) are accessible.
